question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfigured Spring Cloud Config or environment variable injection to set `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled=false`, halting all record file ingestion so that fund transfer transactions are never persisted to the mirror node database, causing users to see stale or missing balances and leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the `setEnabled(false)` path in `Downloader.verifySignatures()` by crafting a record file whose filename timestamp is after `importerProperties.getEndDate()`, causing the downloader to permanently disable itself and stop ingesting all subsequent fund transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a minimum-duration constraint on `frequency` (unlike `CommonDownloaderProperties.pathRefreshInterval` which enforces `@DurationMin(seconds=1)`) to inject `frequency=0ms` via environment variable, causing a tight polling loop that exhausts cloud storage API rate limits and starves legitimate record file downloads, resulting in missed fund transfer transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an extremely large `frequency` value (e.g., `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.frequency=999999d`) via a publicly accessible configuration endpoint, causing the `@Scheduled` downloader to poll so infrequently that fund transfer record files are processed hours or days late, leading to users acting on stale balance data and suffering direct financial loss?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.persistBytes=true` via environment variable injection, causing raw record file bytes (containing full transaction details including transfer amounts and account IDs) to be stored in the database, where a subsequent SQL injection or DB read vulnerability exposes all fund transfer data to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.writeFiles=true` via config injection, causing verified record stream files (containing all fund transfer transactions) to be written to the filesystem via `Utility.archiveFile()`, where world-readable file permissions allow the attacker to read raw transaction data and reconstruct fund flows?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.writeSignatures=true` via config injection, causing all consensus node signature files to be written to the filesystem, exposing node public key usage patterns and enabling an attacker to analyze which nodes signed which record files to identify consensus manipulation opportunities affecting fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getStreamType()` hardcodes `StreamType.RECORD` (with `fileCloseInterval` of 2 seconds) to time a record file injection attack within the 2-second close window, inserting a crafted record file that reports fraudulent fund transfers before the legitimate file is downloaded and verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared `CommonDownloaderProperties` bean (accessed via `getCommon()`) to set `consensusRatio=0` via config injection, causing `ConsensusValidatorImpl` to skip consensus checks entirely for `StreamType.RECORD` files, allowing a single malicious node's record file to be accepted as valid and its fraudulent fund transfers to be persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.downloadRatio=0.01` via environment variable, causing `partialCollection()` to sample only 1% of consensus nodes when downloading record file signatures, making it trivially easy for an attacker controlling even one node to dominate the sampled set and have their fraudulent record file accepted as consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Lombok `@Data`-generated `setEnabled()` setter being public on a Spring `@Component` bean to trigger a race condition where `enabled` is set to `false` mid-batch by the endDate check in `verifySignatures()`, causing partially-processed record files to be skipped and fund transfer transactions to be permanently lost from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a negative `Duration` value for `frequency` (e.g., `frequency=-500ms`) since there is no `@DurationMin` constraint on this field, causing a `NegativeArraySizeException` or scheduler arithmetic overflow in the `@Scheduled(fixedDelayString)` expression, crashing the record file downloader and halting all fund transfer ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `frequency=1ms` (below the 500ms default) via environment variable, causing the record downloader to poll cloud storage at 1000 requests/second, triggering rate limiting or account suspension on the cloud storage provider, which then causes all record file downloads to fail and fund transfer transactions to be missed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between `persistBytes=true` and the `streamFile.setBytes(null)` call in `verifySignatures()` — when `isPersistBytes()` returns `true`, bytes are NOT nulled — to cause oversized record file bytes to be passed to the database parser, triggering a buffer overflow or OOM condition that corrupts the fund transfer record being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `writeFiles=true` combined with a path traversal in `importerProperties.getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()` to write verified record files to an attacker-controlled filesystem path, overwriting existing record files with crafted ones that report different fund transfer amounts when re-read?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `writeSignatures=true` to cause `Utility.archiveFile()` to write all consensus node signature files to disk, then use those signature files to forge a valid-looking signature set for a crafted record file containing fraudulent fund transfers, bypassing the `NodeSignatureVerifier` in a future replay attack?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `StreamType.RECORD` path prefix (`recordstreams`) and the `nodePrefix` (`record`) to enumerate all node record file paths in the cloud storage bucket, then download and analyze unverified record files to front-run fund transfers before they are confirmed on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `enabled=true` is the default and there is no re-enable mechanism once `setEnabled(false)` is called (e.g., after endDate is reached), to craft a scenario where the downloader is permanently disabled after processing a single crafted record file, causing all subsequent fund transfers to be silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared `CommonDownloaderProperties.batchSize=25` (accessed via `getCommon()`) by injecting `batchSize=1` to reduce the number of signature files downloaded per node per batch, causing the downloader to process record files so slowly that fund transfer transactions are delayed by hours, enabling time-sensitive financial attacks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.maxSize=2` (the minimum allowed by `@Min(2L)`) via environment variable, causing the downloader to reject all record files larger than 2 bytes as exceeding `maxSize`, effectively blocking all fund transfer record file ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring Boot Actuator `/actuator/env` endpoint (if misconfigured without authentication) to POST a new value for `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled=false`, disabling record file downloads and causing all fund transfer transactions to be missed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Data`-generated `setEnabled()` being called by `downloadNextBatch()` when the demo network bucket is empty, to craft a scenario where the network name is spoofed to `DEMO` via `hiero.mirror.importer.network=demo`, causing the downloader to disable itself after the first empty poll and permanently stop ingesting mainnet fund transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@NotNull` constraint on `frequency` being the only validation — with no `@DurationMin` — to inject `frequency=PT0S` (zero duration), causing the Spring scheduler to invoke `download()` in a tight loop with zero delay, exhausting thread pool resources and preventing legitimate record file processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `persistBytes=true` combined with a crafted oversized record file (up to `maxSize=50MiB`) to cause the mirror node database to store 50MiB of raw bytes per record file, rapidly exhausting database storage and causing the importer to crash, resulting in a gap in fund transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `allowAnonymousAccess` flag in `CommonDownloaderProperties` (accessed via `getCommon()`) being set to `true` for certain networks, to anonymously download all record files from the cloud storage bucket without authentication, extracting complete fund transfer histories for all accounts?
